calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES CONNOLLY's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-Defendant James Connolly's special motion to strike Cross-Complainant Giga AI's cross-complaint pursuant to California's anti-SLAPP statute is DENIED.

Connolly's objections to exhibits A, B, C & D of the Kyaw Declaration are sustained.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2026 Continued To May-13-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to the hearing, Counsel appears in an abundance of caution. The clerk informs the party the court did not receive any notice to contest the court's TR. The Court's TR will be the final ruling of the Court.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GIGA AI, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Defendant Giga AI, Inc.'s unopposed Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.)

Good cause appearing, the court grants Giga AI, Inc. leave to file the proposed cross-complaint.

Giga AI, Inc. must file its cross-complaint, substantially identical to the attachment to the motion, within ten days of entry of this order.

Prior to the hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
